label: Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization

node.label: Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization
node.definition: Multiple competing goals and hard constraints are optimized jointly rather than collapsed into one unconstrained score.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization is modeled as depending on Optimization theory, Game theory & decision theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment.
examples: atlas downstream link to Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Optimization theory, Game theory & decision theory.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization has a `uses` dependency on Game theory & decision theory because Game theory & decision theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization has a `uses` dependency on Labels, targets, rewards & preferences because Labels, targets, rewards & preferences is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
